title: Build identity record
description: Machine-readable identity of a single workspace build. Emitted by tools/build/print_build_identity.sh and produced beside every artifact of tools/build/build.sh. Governance tooling (provenance, clean-room rebuild verification, crash/symbol lookup) consumes this record.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this record. Increment when fields are added, removed, or have changed meaning.

commit.description: Full git commit hash the build was produced from, or 'unknown' if not in a git checkout.

commit_short.description: 12-character prefix of 'commit', convenience field for human-readable logs.

dirty.description: True if the working tree had uncommitted changes. Release builds must reject dirty trees; dev builds tolerate them.

toolchain_channel.description: Contents of rust-toolchain.toml [toolchain].channel, e.g. '1.84.0'. Must match rustc_version.

host_triple.description: rustc -vV host triple of the machine that ran the build.

target_triple.description: Target triple the artifacts were built for. Defaults to host_triple when no cross-target was specified.

profile.description: Cargo profile the artifacts were built with.

source_date_epoch.description: Deterministic build epoch. Defaults to the committer timestamp of HEAD; callers may pin it by exporting SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH before invocation.

build_timestamp_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC rendering of source_date_epoch. This is the only fully deterministic build-time field; the wall-clock time of the actual build is intentionally not recorded.
